auth.totpHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
auth.resetSent: If the email exists, a reset link was sent.
auth.passwordUpdatedLogin: Password updated. You can now log in.
auth.noAccessToken: Login succeeded but no access token was returned.
auth.sessionLoadFailed: Logged in but could not load your session. Check the browser console or Railway logs.
auth.totpNoAccessToken: TOTP verified but no access token was returned.
auth.totpSessionLoadFailed: TOTP verified but could not load your session. The API may still be deploying — wait a moment and try again. Check the browser console for details.

settings.title: Profile
settings.lead: Your account details, password, authenticator, and language.
settings.totpEnrollHint: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
settings.languageLead: Choose the language used across the portal for your account.
settings.languageSavedLocally: Language updated on this device. Server settings are not available yet.

errors.network: Network error — could not reach the API. The backend may be redeploying or temporarily unavailable.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.greeting: Hi {{name}} — here’s your cloud spend overview.
dashboard.selectContext: Select a company and connection in the top bar to view daily total cost.
dashboard.topSpendSpikes: Top spend spikes vs recent baseline
dashboard.noSpendSpikes: No spend spikes in the last 30 days.

chart.noSeriesData: No series data for this range.

costExplorer.title: Cost Explorer
costExplorer.lead: Explore daily spend by service, scope, or top resources. Click a service/scope to filter the trend, or a top resource for cost + utilization.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.lead: Where resources cost more than they’re used — with the fix and the savings.

anomalies.title: Cost Anomalies
anomalies.lead: Days where spend jumped well above its recent trend — what changed, by how much, and what drove it.

unitEconomics.title: Unit Economics
unitEconomics.lead: Divide your cloud bill by a business count you care about (customers, seats, orders) to see cost each.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.lead: Synced cloud resources for the selected connection — browse by type and compartment.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.lead: Utilization and performance metrics for resources in the selected connection.

reports.title: Reports
reports.lead: Download a CSV cost report for the selected date range.

connections.title: Connections
connections.lead: Cloud account connections for this company. OCI is available today; AWS and GCP will appear here when their connectors ship.

budgets.title: Budgets & Alerts
budgets.lead: Set spend caps and email preferences. Budgets and alerts are stored on the server.

allocations.title: Allocations
allocations.lead: Map spend to teams and products with tags and chargeback rules. Tag sync is coming next.

dashboard.intro: The dashboard summarizes cloud spend, savings opportunities, and unusual cost changes.
dashboard.howTo1: Select a company and cloud connection from the top bar.
dashboard.howTo2: Choose a cloud and date range to focus the cost view.
dashboard.see1: Total and average daily spend for the selected period.
dashboard.see2: The services and compartments driving the most cost.
dashboard.see3: Active recommendations that may reduce monthly spend.
dashboard.see4: Recent cost spikes compared with the usual baseline.
dashboard.footer: Open any section to investigate its details.

advice.idle: Stop or terminate this instance — you're paying for compute that isn't being used.
advice.oversized: Downsize to a smaller shape (roughly half the OCPUs/memory).
advice.overutilized: Upsize to a larger shape (or scale out) to protect performance.
advice.idleLb: Terminate this load balancer if it's no longer serving traffic.
advice.idleVolume: Review it — if nothing needs this volume, terminate it.
advice.idleBootVolume: Review it — if nothing needs this boot volume, terminate it.
advice.idleFileSystem: Review it — if nothing needs this file system, terminate it.
advice.unattachedBlock: Terminate this block volume if nothing needs the data, or attach it to an instance if it is still required.
advice.unattachedBoot: Terminate this boot volume if nothing needs the data, or attach it to an instance if it is still required.
